title: Pregnancy Due Date Calculator 2026 — EDD by LMP · Weeks | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Pregnancy Due Date Calculator (EDD by LMP)
description: 👶 Free pregnancy calculator — enter your LMP and get your estimated due date (EDD), current gestational week, trimester, and weekly milestones. Week-by-week tra
intro: This pregnancy calculator applies **Naegele's rule** — the standard method used by obstetricians worldwide — to tell you what exact **week and day** of gestation you're in, which **trimester** you're in, what your **estimated due date (EDD)** is, and how many days remain until 40 weeks. All you need to enter is the **date of the first day of your last menstrual period (LMP)**; the formula is `EDD = LMP + 280 days`. It's a valid estimate for regular 28-day cycles; if yours is longer or shorter, or if you had assisted fertilization, a first-trimester ultrasound (weeks 8-12) remains the most accurate method for adjusting the date.
keyTakeaway: The **estimated due date** = LMP + **280 days** (40 weeks). Weeks are counted **from the last menstrual period**, not from conception (which occurs ~2 weeks later). Delivery between **weeks 37 and 42** is considered full-term.
useCases: You just found out you're pregnant and want to know which week you're in.
You're planning maternity leave and need the estimated delivery date.
You want to know which trimester you're in to schedule prenatal checkups.
You're tracking your baby's development week by week.
Your OB/GYN gave you a due date and you want to verify the calculation.

fields.label: Last menstrual period (LMP)
fields.help: First day of your last menstrual period

explanation:
## How Pregnancy Weeks Are Calculated

**Naegele's rule**, created by German obstetrician Franz Naegele in 1812, is the global standard for calculating the estimated due date.

```
EDD = LMP + 280 days
EDD = LMP + 7 days - 3 months + 1 year (mental shortcut)
```

**LMP** = Last Menstrual Period (first day of your last period).

**Mental shortcut example**: if LMP was April 10, 2026 → add 7 days (April 17) - 3 months (January 17) + 1 year = **January 17, 2027**.

The current week is calculated as: `(today - LMP) / 7`, rounding down.

## Why Weeks Count From the Last Period, Not Conception

Although **conception** (fertilization) occurs approximately 2 weeks after LMP (at the time of ovulation in a 28-day cycle), obstetricians count from LMP because it's a **verifiable date** (women remember it), while the exact moment of conception is usually unknown. So when we say "you're 4 weeks pregnant," there's actually only ~2 weeks of embryo.

## Pregnancy Trimesters

| Trimester | Weeks | Days | Key Milestones |
|---|---|---|---|
| **First** | 1-12 | 0-84 | Organ formation, first ultrasound, positive beta-hCG |
| **Second** | 13-26 | 85-182 | Sex visible on ultrasound, feeling baby move (wk 18-22) |
| **Third** | 27-40 | 183-280 | Rapid growth, maternity leave, birth preparation |

## Approximate Baby Size Week by Week

| Weeks | Length | Weight |
|---|---|---|
| 8 | 1.6 cm | 1 g |
| 12 | 5.4 cm | 14 g |
| 16 | 11.6 cm | 100 g |
| 20 | 25 cm | 300 g |
| 24 | 30 cm | 600 g |
| 28 | 37 cm | 1,000 g |
| 32 | 42 cm | 1,700 g |
| 36 | 47 cm | 2,600 g |
| 40 | 51 cm | 3,400 g |

> Average values per the WHO; your baby may be larger or smaller. Only ultrasound provides actual measurements.

## Recommended Prenatal Checkups

- **Week 8-12**: first ultrasound (confirms gestational age and viability).
- **Week 11-13+6**: first trimester screening ultrasound (nuchal translucency).
- **Week 16-20**: lab work (blood count, urinalysis, STD screening).
- **Week 20-22**: **anatomy scan** (morphological ultrasound).
- **Week 24-28**: glucose tolerance test (gestational diabetes).
- **Week 28**: Tdap vaccine.
- **Week 32-34**: growth ultrasound.
- **Week 35-37**: Group B streptococcus culture.
- **Week 37 onward**: weekly checkup, fetal monitoring.

## Full-Term, Preterm, and Post-Term Delivery

| Type | Weeks |
|---|---|
| **Extremely preterm** | <28 |
| **Very preterm** | 28-32 |
| **Late preterm** | 32-37 |
| **Early term** | 37-38+6 |
| **Full term** | 39-40+6 ✅ |
| **Late term** | 41-41+6 |
| **Post-term** | ≥42 |

Only **5% of babies** are born exactly on their due date. 80-90% are born between weeks 37 and 42 (full-term).

## Common Mistakes

1. **Counting from conception**: if you ovulated on the 20th and think you're 4 weeks based on that, your medical pregnancy is actually 6 weeks.
2. **LMP from an irregular cycle**: if your cycle isn't 28 days, Naegele's rule may be off. Use ultrasound instead.
3. **Thinking pregnancy is exactly 9 months**: it's 10 lunar months or 9 months and 1 week (40 weeks).
4. **Believing the due date is fixed**: it's an estimate; only 5% deliver on that exact date.

faq.q: How accurate is the pregnancy calculator?
faq.a: It's an estimate based on **Naegele's rule** assuming a **28-day cycle**. It can vary **±2 weeks** depending on cycle irregularity. A **first-trimester ultrasound (weeks 8-12)** is the most accurate method with a margin of error of ±5 days. This calculator is useful but does not replace prenatal care.

faq.q: When do pregnancy weeks start counting?
faq.a: **From the first day of your last menstrual period (LMP)**, not from fertilization. Conception occurs approximately 2 weeks after LMP in a regular cycle. So when you say 'I'm 4 weeks pregnant,' the actual embryo is about 2 weeks old.

faq.q: How long does a normal pregnancy last?
faq.a: **40 weeks** (280 days) from LMP on average. Any delivery between **weeks 37 and 42** is considered full-term. Before 37 weeks is **preterm**; after 42 is **post-term** (only 5% of pregnancies). Only 5% of babies are born exactly on their due date.

faq.q: What if my cycle is not 28 days?
faq.a: Naegele's rule assumes regular cycles. If your cycle is longer (35 days, for example), ovulation occurs later and the actual pregnancy is a few days less than calculated. For very irregular cycles or IVF pregnancies, a **first-trimester ultrasound** gives you the correct date.

faq.q: Can the due date change after the first ultrasound?
faq.a: **Yes**. If the first-trimester ultrasound shows a measurement different from what was calculated by LMP, obstetricians typically **adjust the EDD to the ultrasound measurement** if the difference is greater than 7 days.

**First (1-12):** confirmation, first ultrasound, beta-hCG, prenatal labs, vitamins. **Second (13-26):** anatomy scan, feeling baby move, belly starts showing. **Third (27-40):** growth monitoring, vaccines, diabetes test, Group B strep culture, and birth preparation.

faq.q: When can you find out the baby's sex?
faq.a: By **ultrasound**, usually between **weeks 18 and 22** (during the anatomy scan). With **NIPT** (non-invasive prenatal test, maternal blood), you can find out from **week 10** with 99% accuracy. In IVF pregnancies with preimplantation testing, the sex is known from implantation.

sources.name: ACOG - How Your Fetus Grows During Pregnancy
Mayo Clinic - Pregnancy Week by Week

example.title: Example: LMP on January 10, 2026, today is April 14
example.result: You're in **week 13+3 of the second trimester**. Your delivery is estimated for **October 17, 2026**. 186 days to go.
